title: Input Enhancement in String Matching: The KMP Preprocessing Flow
description: This flowchart illustrates the process of building the Longest Prefix Suffix (LPS) array, which is the input enhancement step used by the Knuth-Morris-Pratt (KMP) algorithm to optimize string pattern matching.
relationships: The algorithm processes the Pattern String to populate the LPS Array
The Length (len) and Index (i) variables track positions for comparison
Comparisons between characters determine whether to increment 'len' or reset it using previous LPS values
The final LPS Array is the enhanced input used for the efficient main KMP search
